title: How to hide "+" column, expand text fields on shared board
excerpt:
When sharing a board with the public, is there a way to hide the final “+” column that shows up? I am not seeing any way to remove this. I’ve hidden other columns we don’t want public, but this one is still showing. 
Add…

posts.author: mondayuser6 (Jim)
posts.cookedHtml:
When sharing a board with the public, is there a way to hide the final “+” column that shows up? I am not seeing any way to remove this. I’ve hidden other columns we don’t want public, but this one is still showing.

Additionally, in the text fields that do show, is there a way to show the entire text content without someone clicking into it? It’s not intuitive that there is more content.

Example: Test Share

image1641×452 21.8 KB

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml:
I don’t know of a way to remove the ‘plus’.

image851×482 16.3 KB

For the column, what if you do a double or triple height?

image851×482 35.8 KB

Desiree

thecleverclovers.com

Authorized monday.com Partner

Authorized monday.com Partner

posts.author: mondayuser6 (Jim)
posts.cookedHtml: I did try the double/triple height. The height does not seem to affect the shared view, and if it did, that only helps for two- or three-line content. The same issue would be in place for anything longer.

posts.author: MASO (Markus @ Gorilla Apps)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Jim,

I doubt you can get rid of the (+) or do customization that is reflected on the public shared view. It’s kind of a fixed template in my opinion.

That’s why we built Board to Website Widget, which lets you share and embed boards just the way you want. Here is an example: Shared Board vs Embedded Widget

Though it’s a paid third party app.

Cheers,
Markus

posts.author: mondayuser6 (Jim)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks, Markus. As a non-profit, we try to stay away from paid add-ons if we can. Those all start adding up. That’s the biggest drawback for us with Monday.com, that so many requests are only solved with third-party options.
